zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William Douglas of Lochleven於1382年出生在英國蘇格蘭金洛-爾金洛斯時，他的父親Henry Douglas of Lugton and Lochleven35歲，母親Marjory Stewart47歲。他於1409年與Marjory Elizabeth Lindsay結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1421年3月21日在法國帕斯 德 拉 洛易薩特波奧勒享年39歲。

nameMeanings.name: Douglas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from any of various places called from their situation on a river named with Gaelic dubh ‘dark, black’ + glas ‘stream’ (a derivative of glas ‘blue’). There are several localities in Scotland and Ireland so named, but the one from which the surname is derived in most if not all cases is Douglas in Lanarkshire 20 miles south of Glasgow, the original stronghold of the influential Douglas family and their retainers.

History: The family taking their name from Douglas in Lanarkshire were of Flemish origin. They rose to great prominence in the 14th and 15th centuries, controlling the earldoms of Douglas, Morton, and Angus, and later, Queensberry.

variants.name: Douglass
meanings: Scottish and English (Durham and Northumbria): variant of   Douglas  .

History: William Douglass, a physician recognized for his identification and description of an epidemic of scarlet fever, was born c. 1691 in Gifford, Haddington County, Scotland, and settled in Boston in 1718. The abolitionist, orator, and journalist Frederick Douglass assumed the name after escaping from slavery in 1838 and traveling to Massachusetts. Son of a white father and a slave with some Indian blood, he was born Frederick Augustus Washington Bailey c. 1817 in Tuckahoe, MD.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William Douglas of Lochleven was born in 1382, in Kinross, Kinross-shire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Henry Douglas of Lugton and Lochleven, was 35 and his mother, Marjory Stewart, was 47. He married Marjory Elizabeth Lindsay in 1409. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 21 March 1421, in Beauge, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France, at the age of 39.

Il a épousé Marjory Elizabeth Lindsay en 1409. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 21 mars 1421, à Beauge, Sarthe, Pays de la Loire, France, à l’âge de 39 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir William Douglas of Lochleven(이)가 1382년 영국 스코틀랜드 킨로스-실르 킨로스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Douglas of Lugton and Lochleven(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Marjory Stewart(은)는 47세였다. 그는 1409년 Marjory Elizabeth Lindsay(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1421년 3월 21일 프랑스 파이스 데 라 로이르 사르테 보지에서 39세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William Douglas of Lochleven родился в 1382 г. в местности кинросс, кинросс-шир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Henry Douglas of Lugton and Lochleven было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Marjory Stewart, 47. Он женился на женщине по имени Marjory Elizabeth Lindsay в 1409 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 21 марта 1421 г. в местности бодж, сарт, пеис де ла лоир, Франция в возрасте 39 лет.
